title: Layer resolving numerical scheme for singularly perturbed parabolic convection-diffusion problem with an interior layer
author: Kusi, Habte, et al.

steps.title: Stability of the method
steps.instruction: To investigate the stability estimate for the formulated method, the Von Neumann stability technique is applied to investigate the stability of Eq. (13), by assuming that the solution of this equation at the grid point (xm,tn) is given by:(14)Umn=ξneimθ,where i=−1, with θ is the real number and ξ denotes the amplitude factor. Substituting Eq. (14) into the homogeneous part of Eq. (13) and then solve ξ given as:ξ=−cmnk[2εhm(hm+hm+1)−amnhm]e−iθ+−2εhmhm+1+amnhm−bmn−1kcmn+2εhm(hm+hm+1)eiθ

steps.title: Stability of the method
steps.instruction: From the definition problem within the conditions in Eq. (3), we have:min∀(x,t)|a(x,t)|=0,b(x,t)≥b0≥0.

steps.title: Stability of the method
steps.instruction: This condition also works for the discrete one and hence|ξ|≤1, which implies the developed finite difference method in Eq. (13) is unconditionally stable.
